metadata.title: Build a Cross-Model Sharpe Ratio Verifier with Claude

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Watching NVDA's Sharpe slide from 1.52 to 1.38 by switching root-252 to root-12 convinced me the convention is doing the work. I want to map all four choices at once. Help me: (1) build a grid that varies window, frequency, return type, and risk-free rate and prints a Sharpe for each combination; (2) rank which single choice moves NVDA's number the most; (3) find the convention combination that would flip the NVDA-versus-SPY recommendation so I can argue about the assumption, not the number.

props.command: Watching NVDA's Sharpe slide from 1.52 to 1.38 by switching root-252 to root-12 convinced me the convention is doing the work. I want to map all four choices at once. Help me: (1) build a grid that varies window, frequency, return type, and risk-free rate and prints a Sharpe for each combination; (2) rank which single choice moves NVDA's number the most; (3) find the convention combination that would flip the NVDA-versus-SPY recommendation so I can argue about the assumption, not the number.
props.segment: Build a Cross-Model Sharpe Ratio Verifier

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

lines: Three models, three answers. Which one is right?
Build it. Verify it. Break it.

beats.role_note: the problem, context, stakes -- BEFORE the CLI opens
beats.narration_text: Three models return NVDA Sharpes of 1.38, 1.41, and 1.62 for the same ticker — the spread is 15% and could flip a recommendation. The Sharpe ratio is not a fact. It is a fact conditional on four choices: window, frequency, return type, and risk-free rate. Any one of those choices can move the number enough to change the story.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- title card: THREE MODELS, ONE TICKER, THREE ANSWERS. The spread is the hidden assumption.

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude to write the verifier script — NVDA versus SPY, both windows, every convention labeled in every header.

props.command: claude "Write a Python script using yfinance that computes NVDA vs SPY: arithmetic return, CAGR, annualized volatility (daily, scaled by √252), and Sharpe ratio at the 3-month T-bill rate, for both a 3-year and 5-year window. Print a labelled markdown table and save a CSV. Include the convention in every header: frequency, window, return type."

beats.narration_text: The script pulls yfinance monthly returns, scales volatility by root-252, and prints convention labels on every header — frequency, window, return type.

props.code:
import yfinance as yf, numpy as np, pandas as pd
from datetime import date, timedelta

def sharpe(returns, rf_annual=0.045):
    rf_daily = (1+rf_annual)**(1/252)-1
    excess = returns - rf_daily
    return excess.mean()/excess.std() * np.sqrt(252)

for window_yrs in [3, 5]:
    start = date.today() - timedelta(days=365*window_yrs)
    nvda = yf.download('NVDA', start=start)['Close'].pct_change().dropna()
    spy  = yf.download('SPY',  start=start)['Close'].pct_change().dropna()
    print(f"|Window={window_yrs}yr|NVDA Sharpe={sharpe(nvda):.2f}|SPY={sharpe(spy):.2f}|vol×√252|")

beats.role_note: first output -- Manim animated bar chart
beats.narration_text: NVDA's 3-year Sharpe lands at 1.52, SPY at 0.52. The 5-year window compresses NVDA to 1.15 — the AI rally is a 3-year story. The bar chart makes that visible: two grouped pairs, each bar labeled with its convention.
beats.visual_intent: Manim B04_SharpeBar: two grouped bar charts comparing NVDA vs SPY across 3-year and 5-year windows. AI rally annotation arrow on the NVDA 3-year bar.

beats.narration_text: Now change the frequency assumption: compute volatility from monthly returns scaled by root-12 instead of daily returns scaled by root-252. One line changes — the number changes.

props.command: claude "Modify the script: recompute volatility from monthly returns scaled by √12 instead of daily returns scaled by √252. Print both the daily-vol Sharpe and the monthly-vol Sharpe in the same table, with a SPREAD column showing the difference."

beats.narration_text: The monthly-vol Sharpe for NVDA 3-year drops from 1.52 to about 1.38 — a 9% difference from one frequency choice. The SPREAD column makes the hidden assumption visible: the convention, not the market, is doing the work.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- screen-recording: terminal table showing daily-vol Sharpe vs monthly-vol Sharpe with SPREAD column; NVDA row shows ~0.14 spread highlighted.

beats.narration_text: The Sharpe ratio is not a fact — it is a fact conditional on four choices. Window, frequency, return type, risk-free rate. Any one of those choices can move the number enough to change the recommendation. The verifier names the choices so you can argue about the convention instead of the number.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- recap card: SHARPE IS CONDITIONAL. Four choices, four ways to change the story. Name them.

beats.narration_text: Next: try recomputing with monthly returns scaled by root-12 and compare to the daily-volatility number. Follow for more computational finance builds.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- next-steps card: Try monthly vol (x√12) vs daily vol (x√252). The spread is the convention premium.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Here's what the body just demonstrated. The Sharpe ratio is not a fact — it is a fact conditional on four choices. Window, frequency, return type, risk-free rate. Any one of those choices can move the number enough to change the recommendation. The verifier names the choices so you can argue about the convention instead of the number. Next: try recomputing with monthly returns scaled by root-12 and compare to the daily-volatility number. Follow for more computational finance builds.
